properties.remark: Reports have been received of a mud slide blocking Paint Creek Rd just south of the Turnpike exit.

Wayne County 911 Center has received reports of a rockslide in the 4000 block of 5th Street Rd, near Huntington Gravel & Sand, north of Lavalette. Three motor vehicles were involved. No injuries reported.

Wayne County 911 Center has reports of a rockslide at the intersection of Round Bottom Rd and Centerville Rd near the town of Prichard, WV. One lane of travel is blocked and a law enforcement vehicle was involved. No injuries reported.
Raleigh County 911 Center received reports of a rock slide that occurred on Clear Fork Rd, just below Fulton Bottom and blocking one lane of travel heading towards Whitesville.
Trees uprooted and damaged on Flint Ridge Rd and by Crooksville School.

A NWS survey team found that strong straight line winds of 80 to 90 mph resulted in tree damage to a mixture of softwood and hardwood trees just south of Crooksville. Trees were either uprooted or snapped off, all lying in a northeast direction.
EM passed along security camera footage of what looks like quarter sized hail falling.
Multiple lines down blocking County Road 1.
Tree reported down blocking Hazelgreen Road.
Tree down between Foreman Drive and Gabe Road.
Perry Branch out of its banks and flooding State Route 2078.
Tree down on Scott Depot Rd. U.S. 60 in Scott Depot.
TREE FELL ACROSS THE LINE, SNAPPED THE POLE.
Tree down blocking the right side of the road due to high wind.
EM of Nitro reported Belies Drive and 39th Street East underwater.
Armour Creek out of its bank with water surrounding mobile home on the end of 39th Street East. The street is also flooded in that area.
Perry Branch out of its banks and flooding Hall Lane.
Dry Fork out of its banks and flooding junction of Dry Fork and Aden Road.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 5 WNW Audra State Park. Tree down partially blocking the road at 2220 Teter Rd, Buckannon.
Tree down partially blocking the road at 2220 Teter Rd, Buckannon.
There are 2 trees down in Elbert at the 39 load out. The trees are blocking both lanes of traffic.
Tree reported down on Coal Heritage Road blocking both lanes.

LINES REPORTED DOWN ON MAINESVILLE ROAD.
Tree fell on vehicle in Malta Hill. Time estimated by radar.
TREE FELL ON VEHICLE IN MALTA HILL. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
2 large pine trees in the road at Buffalo Mt Rd and Pine Grove Rd.
Trained spotter reports lots of nickel sized hail and several a bit bigger than a quarter. Strong winds as well.
County 911 reported two large trees down on Norvell Drive in Buckhannon. Time estimated from radar.
Report from mPING: Quarter (1.00 in.).
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 2 SSW Buckhannon. 2 large trees down blocking the road.
County 911 center reports tree down on Shilo Rd near Moatsville.
2 large trees down blocking the road.
Path of damage begins at Olive Hill and continues eastward paralleling I-64 to Grayson.

Updates previous report. Intermittent path of damage begins near Olive Hill and continues eastward paralleling I-64 to near Grayson. Tree damage and some minor structure damage was noted. Power poles were damaged at exit 161 causing power lines to fall onto I-64. This shut down the interstate for a period of time and damaged a tractor trailer.
Upon assessment of drone footage, the Greenup Ky to Ironton OH Microburst has been reclassified as a high end EF1 tornado with maximum winds of up to 110 mph.
Multiple trees down in multiple locations in eastern part of county.
A couple buildings heavily damaged in the fairgrounds with widespread tree damage in the city of Greenup.

Swath of damage consistent with a microburst noted from Greenup KY to the west side of Ironton OH. The most significant damage occurred near Hanging Rock where several travel trailers were overturned and significant tree damage was noted. Estimated wind speeds of 90 mph.
Ironton Country Club club house roof was torn away and doors of the cart house were blown off.
Trees down on Oakmont Manor.
Several campers flipped over and structures damaged.
Wind gust measured on a Davis weather station mounted on the roof of the Boyd County EOC. Several buildings in the industrial park also have garage doors blown in and have missing or damaged roofs.

An EF1 tornado with estimated maximum winds of up to 110 mph skipped eastward along a 1.35 mile path embedded in straight-line wind damage. The tornado likely began along Daniels Fork Rd, continuing across State Route 5, continuing eastward crossing Rose Road and then ending at the top of the hill after crossing Pine St. The most significant damage occurred along Rose Rd where a single-wide trailer was flipped over, a section of a home was broken off from the rest of the structure and moved several feet, and several other homes and structures were significantly damaged.
Several trees snapped or uprooted along State Route 141 near Sugar Creek Christian Academy.

Photos submitted by Boyd County EMA clearly indicate tornadic damage of at least EF1 intensity with two mobile homes displaced and/or rolled, trees uprooted and snapped along with additional structural damage on Rose Road. A damage survey will take place at a later time to assess path width, length and final EF rating.

An EF2 tornado with estimated maximum winds of up to 120 mph occurred along Long Branch Road. The tornado caused considerable and significant tree damage with several large hardwoods snapped and uprooted. A home was severely damaged when the roof was blown off, and other homes also suffered at least minor damage. Several barns and outbuildings were destroyed. Access issues limited the assessment of the track ending point and track length is estimated at this time.
Trees and power lines down on Lowman Dr and Leighwood Dr.
Trailer overturned and houses nearby destroyed.

Boyd County Emergency Management drone footage shows a quarter-mile long path of uprooted and snapped trees associated with microburst winds of up to 100 mph along Southern Hills Drive. Several homes suffered damaged from the fallen trees.

An EF1 tornado with maximum winds of up to 90 mph began near Aid-Oak Ridge Rd and traveled northeastward through mostly pastureland, knocking down a few trees on the north edge of the path and where it crossed Symmes Creek. The tornado continued northeast, blowing the roof off of two homes and another structure and rolling a travel trailer near where it crossed State Route 141. The last noted damage was some tin panels blown up on a barn roof shortly after the tornado crossed State Route 141.
Widespread tree and power line damage. Multiple structures with damage. Truck overturned on Proctorville Bridge.
Several trees down and structures damaged in Aid Township.
Multiple power poles snapped midway up along North Kenova Rd.

A tornado, basically a strong eddy at the end of a microburst, occurred along 5th Street Road. Tree and structural damage in this valley showed strong rotation with damage consistent with an EF1 tornado. Several structures were damaged or destroyed and extensive tree damage occurred. The tornado climbed the ridge to the east of 5th Street Road and dissipated.
A short duration EF1 tornado confirmed with maximum winds of 105 mph in the vicinity of Timber Ridge Lake northwest of Crown City.
Trees and power lines down all over Huntington. Tractor trailor flipped over on the 31st Bridge.
Proctorville Fire Department building destroyed with severe damage to other structures.
Significant damage reported across the town with roofs blown off, 1-2 ft diameter trees down, rear half of fire dept destroyed.
Part of the roof of elementary school blown off.

Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report and location from Wayne. EM reports multiple trees down including a tree down on a home on State Street and Lakeside Drive. Tree damage indicates that it was from straight-line winds.
Rear half of Proctorville FD destroyed. Roof partially removed from Fairland West Elementary School. Damage consistent with microburst. Estimated wind speeds of 90 mph.
Tree down on a home.
Damage consistent with a microburst occurred along Wilson Creek. Several trees were uprooted and some snapped. The roof was also blown off of one house.
A short duration EF1 tornado confirmed with winds to 105 mph. The overall track was just south of Route 2 to the Guyandotte River before lifting just west of the Barboursville Mall.
Out buildings destroyed and several trees down, some onto structures.
A short duration EF1 tornado with maximum winds of 95 mph confirmed along Hannan Trace Road.
Roof and exterior walls of a wood frame commercial warehouse were blown off along Hannan Trace Road.
Structural damage to an exterior wall attached to the Barboursville Mall. Roof damage to a pet supply store in the mall area as well.
Large billboard blown over by I-64.
Tree damage consistent with a microburst on ridgetop along Sand Gap Road.
Structures damage and trees down in Hamlin and up Rt 34 toward Hurricane.
A EF1 tornado confirmed south of Eleanor to Bancroft with a non-continuous path and maximum winds of 90 mph.
Portion of a gas station canopy blown off in Buffalo, WV. Time estimated by radar.
Sheet metal roof blown nearly entirely off in Hurricane.
A short duration EF0 tornado confirmed with maximum winds of 80 mph in the Buffalo area.
Tree down onto a house on Meadowbrook Circle.
Path of tree damage consistent with a microburst occurred along Route 214 between Yawkey and Sumerco Hill.
Multiple trees down in Red House and Bancroft.
Trees down from Scott Depot to Nitro.
Multiple trees down between Yawkey and Sumerco.
Wooden fence blown down. Time estimated by radar.
Multiple large trees down along the Coal River. 41 mph reported on Davis station.

EF1 tornado with maximum winds of 100 mph began in the St. Albans Park. Based on a combination of drone footage and ground surveys it was determined that the tornado skipped eastward, embedded in an larger swath of straight-line wind damage, from Saint Albans to Jefferson to Dunbar and ending in the Roxalana area. This was a non-continuous path with several noted areas of concentrated damage.
Multiple sheet metal roofs damaged, a power pole snapped, and multiple trees down.
Several trees down on WV Tech Park campus. Weather station recorded 56 mph wind gust.
Narrow swath of significant tree damage near Running Right Way off of US119.
Small area of uprooted and snapped trees consistent with a microburst on east side of US 119 at Running Right Way.

EF2 Tornado with maximum winds of 115 mph confirmed with a track of about 8 miles extending from Cross Lanes, West Virginia to just south of the community of Wallace, West Virginia. This was a non-continuous path of with three notable damage locations.
Two of the four WVSR-AM radio towers collapsed. These are approximately 400 ft tall.
Multiple trees snapped off. Time estimated by radar.
Portions of a sheet metal roof ripped off a mobile home.
Multiple trees uprooted.
Adds information to previous tstm wnd dmg report from 1 SSW Dunbar. Large billboard collapsed and tractor trailer blown over on I-64 bridge.
Large billboard collapsed.
Multiple trees down along six mile rd.
Corrects previous non-tstm wnd gst report from 6 E Ravenswood. Mesonet station DRFW2 Drift Run.
Multiple trees and power lines down across northern end of county.
Trees down on Limestone Hill Rd.
Several trees down in Cross Lanes.
Multiple trees down in Danville.

An EF1 tornado touched down in the Kanawha State Forest near the Pigeon Roost Trail then skipped along an intermittent path eastward to the Hernshaw community. Significant tree damage was noted between Hernshaw and Marmet, the top floor was blown off of one structure, an RV was rolled and additional structure damage occurred. The tornado dissipated before reaching I-64.
Trees down between Chapmanville and Logan.
Utility pole snapped and power lines down.
Several large limbs down. Time estimated by radar.
Tree down blocking the roadway.
Tree down on a car.
Trees down.
Powerlines down onto vehicle.
Several trees snapped on hillside along Campbells Creek Rd.
Multiple trees and large limbs down in Kanawha City.
A short, non-continuous EF1 tornado path was observed west of the town of Quick.
Brick facade of commercial building partially destroyed.
Two vehicles destroyed by fallen trees in the South Hills area.
Yeager Airport.
An EF1 tornado snapped and uprooted trees in a distinct path down a hillside into the valley in the Ronda community near Cabin Creek. A few homes suffered roof damage along Ronda Rd.
Trees and lines down along Remington Dr.
Tree and lines down on Calm School Rd blocking the road.
Trees down in Melville along Rt 17.
Structures damaged and a tree down on a house.
Several trees down around town.
Widespread trees down in Bickmore community.

Confirmed microburst with maximum winds of 100 mph in the communities of Jodie and Swiss. along the Nicholas and Fayette County line. Notable mixed hardwood and softwood tree damage occurred along with a roof blown off of one home and an attached car port destroyed from another home.
Several trees snapped on hillside along US 60.
Metal roof removed, power lines on house.

Tree down blocking Sherwood Rd.

Several trees, mostly pine, uprooted or snapped on Dove Ln and Elkhorn Ln. One tree fell onto a house damaging the roof. Damage indicative of a microburst.
Small area with multiple uprooted hardwood trees near intersection of Leivasy Rd and Odell Rd indicative of a microburst.
Multiple trees down from Frametown to Gassaway and Sutton.
Roughly 500 yard long swath of uprooted hardwood trees along Laurel Creek. Tree damage was divergent, indicative of microburst winds blowing oddly from south to north.
Tree down along Rock House Rd.
part of a roof blown off and a window broken out.

Quarter to golf-ball sized hail in Adena Village.

An EF2 tornado began west of Mallard Rd then traveled eastward along Mallard Rd causing considerable and significant tree damage with hundreds of hardwood trees uprooted and snapped. Several homes were damaged by trees or had wind damage along Mallard Rd, including one home that had significant roof damage. The tornado made a slight jog to the northeast crossing Lookout Rd and US 60 before lifting. Peak wind speeds were likely reached just west of Lookout Road where tree damage indicated wind speeds reached an estimated 130 mph. The tornado damaged or destroyed several homes and businesses along US 60. One home was shifted several feet off its foundation and the metal roof was tossed approximately 350 yards to the tree line across US 60.
One structure damaged with person trapped inside.
*** 2 INJ *** Adds additional information to previous report. Several structures damaged along Rt 60 between Lookout and Hico.
Time estimated by radar.
Picture provided via email. Time estimated from radar.
Sporadic tree damage along Snow Hill Rd with minor roof damage noted to a home.
Small area of uprooted trees at intersection of Snow Hill Rd and Leivasy Rd indicative of a microburst.
Report from mPING: Half Dollar (1.25 in.).
Corrects previous non-tstm wnd gst report from 9 WSW Gassaway. Mesonet station DIGW2 Dingy.
70 foot pine tree fell on house. Time estimated from radar.

The tornado began along Erwin Hollow Rd where several hardwood trees were snapped and uprooted. The tornado then moved northeast over a thick forest of mixed pine and hardwood trees between Erwin Hollow Rd and Roberts Rd where drone footage shows an extensive area of leveled hardwood trees and EF2 intensity was obtained with maximum estimated winds of up to 115 mph. The drone footage and ground surveys then show that the tornado weakened and the path became intermittent as it crossed Roberts Road, where a house was damaged when a tree feel on it. The last damage was noted along Oakland Road where several large pine trees were uprooted or snapped.
Tree and power lines reported down near the intersection of Oakland Road and Rock Run Road.
Tree reported down near the intersection of Oakland Road and Roberts Road.

Smith Creek out of its banks and covering Smith Creek Road and into front yard. Caller said this is one of the worst instances of high water in 60 years of that property in the family. Flood waters appeared to be receding from yard and road at time of call, 824 pm.

*** 1 INJ *** EF1 tornado confirmed east of Parkersburg, between State Route 31 and State Highway 50. Areas of narrow but significant tree damage was noted with trees either uprooted or snapped close to their bases in a converging pattern. The highest rated damage occurred along Jericho Road where, in addition to tree damage, a mobile home and barn were flipped over with one minor injury noted.
